rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical tandem thiocyanation/cyclization of N-allylthioamides with NH4SCN to afford thiocyanato-substituted thiazolines (and thiazines from cyclic substrates)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of SCN- to (SCN)2, which acts as an electrophilic thiocyanating mediator toward the alkene; cathode reduces protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: thiocyanogen (SCN)2 generated by anodic oxidation of SCN-
thiiranium/sulfonium intermediate A from (SCN)2 addition to alkene of N-allylthioamide
thiazoline product 3 (or thiazine 4 from cyclic alkene) after intramolecular nucleophilic ring-opening by thioamide sulfur and deprotonation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes two SCN- to (SCN)2 with loss of 2 e-; (SCN)2 adds to the alkene of the N-allylthioamide to form a cyclic sulfonium intermediate A bearing a pendant SCN; the thioamide sulfur opens A intramolecularly and loss of a proton gives the thiazoline (or thiazine), while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction not inhibited; 3a still formed in 68% yield

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction not inhibited; 3a obtained in 57% yield

probe_reagents.name: cyclopropyl-substituted allylthioamide 1ag (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: Product 3ag obtained in 72% yield with cyclopropyl group intact, excluding a thiyl radical intermediate on the substrate

notes: CV showed oxidation peaks for 1a at 1.67 V and for NH4SCN at 1.15 V, indicating SCN- is preferentially oxidized at the anode—supporting a (SCN)2-mediator pathway rather than direct substrate oxidation. The lack of TEMPO/BHT inhibition and the retention of the cyclopropyl ring in the radical-clock experiment argue against a free thiyl/N-centered radical pathway on the substrate, favoring the polar (SCN)2/sulfonium mechanism shown in Scheme 4.
